perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all reasonable and prudent precautions to prevent the Confiden tial Information from reaching third parties. Recipient shall use the Confidential Informati on exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence. Recipient shall use the Confidential Information exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and shall take all reasonable and prudent precautions to prevent the Confidential Information from reaching third parties' is removed. This creates an internal contradiction. While the Recipient is still obligated to hold the information in confidence, the specific requirement to take precautions is removed, making the obligation less enforceable and contradictory to the spirit of maintaining confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement sha ll not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written. Th ese obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient or was or is disclosed to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient, that does not have a nondisclosure oblig ation with respect to it. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient 'shall take all reasonable and prudent precautions to prevent the Confidential Information from reaching third parties.' This clause establishes a clear obligation on the Recipient. The changed text removes this obligation, while still requiring the Recipient to hold the information in strict confidence. The 'Contradicted Text' outlines exceptions to the Recipient's obligations when information becomes public through no fault of their own. By removing the specific obligation to take precautions, the changed text increases the likelihood of information becoming public (e.g., due to negligence), potentially contradicting the spirit of the 'Contradicted Text' because the Recipient is no longer explicitly required to prevent such disclosures. The removal of the preventative measure directly affects the conditions under which the recipient is no longer held liable for confidentiality breaches as described in the Contradicted Text, thus introducing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement sha ll not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written. Th ese obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient or was or is disclosed to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient, that does not have a nondisclosure oblig ation with respect to it. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential In formation Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under la w, regulation or lawful administrative decision.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the first two sentences relating to exceptions for publicly available or previously known information, contradicts the initial commitment to confidentiality. The remainder of the paragraph, dealing only with legally mandated disclosure, creates a significant gap in protection for the disclosing party. The obligation to protect that information is no longer there, and the entire document is undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Informa tion and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information. The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided exceptions to the confidentiality obligations for information that was already publicly available or known to the Recipient. The changed text removes these exceptions, significantly narrowing the scope of permissible disclosures. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that delivery of confidential information does not relieve the Recipient of its confidentiality obligations. With the original exceptions removed, the recipient is now obligated to maintain the confidentiality of information they previously had no obligation to protect, creating a direct and strong contradiction. The explanation reinforces this point, noting the gap in protection created by the omission.

perturbation.original_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Informa tion and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information. The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request, Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by th e respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other repr oductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The sentence 'The delivery of such mate rial shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.' has been removed. This creates an omission that contradicts the overarching purpose of the NDA because it suggests that returning the information might absolve the recipient of their confidentiality obligations. This creates uncertainty about the recipient's long-term duty to protect the information even after returning it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009. The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of five (5) years thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the sentence stating that delivering the confidential information does not relieve the recipient of their confidentiality obligations. The contradicted text states that the confidentiality obligations remain valid for five years after the agreement ends. The removal of the sentence implies that returning the material might absolve the recipient of their confidentiality obligations, which directly contradicts the stated five-year obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009. The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of five (5) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall come into force after signatu re of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28, 2009.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreemen t for a period of five (5) years thereafter.', the clause pertaining to confidentiality after the expiry of the agreement is removed. This omission now contradicts the inherent purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement, which typically extends confidentiality obligations beyond the agreement's termination date, creating uncertainty regarding the long-term protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence. Recipient shall use the Confidential Information exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that obligations under Article 2 (presumably containing confidentiality clauses) remain valid for five years after the agreement's expiry. The changed text removes this clause, implying that confidentiality obligations cease upon the agreement's expiration. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and use it exclusively for specific purposes, without specifying an expiration. By removing the post-termination validity, the modified text creates a contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, aligning with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
